semantic.role: Documentation of the automated stale issue and pull request triage workflow for the ArchSpine repository.
responsibilities: Defines the schedule (daily at 01:30 UTC) and triggers for the stale triage action.
Sets thresholds: 60 days of inactivity to mark stale, 14 more days to close.
Configures labels: applies 'stale' label to issues and PRs; exempts 'security' and 'on-deck' labels.
Provides the message posted when marking an item as stale.
outOfScope: Manual triage processes or human-in-the-loop workflows.
Other automation rules (e.g., auto-labeling, assignment, or comment moderation).
Handling of non-issue/non-PR items (e.g., discussions, milestones).

English.purpose: This document describes the automated workflow that helps keep the repository clean by identifying and closing stale issues and pull requests after periods of inactivity.
English.context_and_audience: The primary audience is repository maintainers and contributors who need to understand how and when issues/PRs are automatically marked stale and eventually closed. It is also relevant for anyone submitting issues or PRs, so they know the expected timeline for activity and closure.
key_takeaways: Issues and PRs with no activity for 60 days are automatically labeled 'stale' and receive a warning message.
After 14 more days without activity, they are automatically closed.
Items labeled 'security' or 'on-deck' are exempt from this process.
The workflow runs nightly at 1:30 AM UTC.
